summary: Iteration 6 PDF export testing. Backend: 7/7 pytest tests pass — GET /api/incidents/{id} returns full dict with all required fields, GET /api/incidents/{id}/pdf returns 200 application/pdf with correct Content-Disposition (Safelyn_Incident_{name}_{ref}.pdf), PDF content (pypdf-extracted) includes all required sections (Safelyn Systems, INCIDENT REPORT, YOUNG PERSON, LOGGED BY, REFERENCE, STRUCTURED REPORT, VERIFICATION, REPORTING STAFF, REVIEWED BY (MANAGER), 2x Signature: lines, 2x Date: lines, #8-char ref, SAFEGUARDING FLAGGED, tag names, author name), unauth→401, non-existent→404. Frontend: IncidentDetail page renders correctly on desktop+mobile with all data-testids (incident-detail-page, detail-timestamp, detail-author, detail-ref starting with #, detail-structured-report, download-pdf-btn). download-pdf-btn triggers fetch to /api/incidents/{id}/pdf and anchor download with suggested_filename='Safelyn_Incident_*_*.pdf'. Incidents list has 21 view-incident-{id} + download-pdf-{id} buttons — view navigates to /incidents/{id}, download triggers PDF download. Mobile 390px: no horizontal overflow (sw=cw=390), download button at y=89 (top of page). LogIncident save-receipt-download-pdf button code verified (downloadSavedPdf function identical to detail page's).
critical_code_review_comments: Backend PDF endpoint and detail endpoint correctly exclude _id projection and require auth via get_current_user. PDF builder (~460 lines) is a single-purpose module — borderline but acceptable since it's cohesive.

Minor UX note (not a bug): When the frontend cannot find the resident in the residents list (e.g. legacy incident whose resident was deleted), downloadPdf in IncidentDetail.jsx and LogIncident.jsx falls back to filename 'Safelyn_Incident_incident_{REF}.pdf'. The backend Content-Disposition actually sends the correct resident name, but the frontend anchor.download attribute overrides the server filename. Consider honoring the server-provided filename from Content-Disposition for full correctness. Doesn't affect acceptance criteria.
downloadSavedPdf in LogIncident.jsx and downloadPdf in IncidentDetail.jsx are near-duplicates (>20 LOC each). Consider extracting to a shared lib/pdf.js helper (DRY).
GET /api/incidents/{iid}/pdf uses user.get('name', '—') for generated_for audit footer — verified present in PDF text. Good.
test_credentials: staff@care.local/Staff@123, manager@care.local/Manager@123 — both used and working
seed_data_creation: Created 1 TEST_PDF_Young_Person resident + 1 safeguarding incident with tags/structured_report via backend_test for PDF content verification (cleaned up in teardown_module).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All acceptance criteria met. PDF content verified via pypdf: contains 'Safelyn Systems', 'INCIDENT REPORT', resident name, author_name, 'YOUNG PERSON', 'LOGGED BY', 'REFERENCE', 'STRUCTURED REPORT', 'VERIFICATION', 'REPORTING STAFF', 'REVIEWED BY (MANAGER)', 2x 'Signature:' lines, 2x 'Date:' lines, '#' + 8-char ref, 'SAFEGUARDING FLAGGED', tag names ('aggression', 'restraint'). Frontend: Detail page data-testids all present and populated; download-pdf-btn triggers correct fetch + anchor download. List page has view-incident-{id} and download-pdf-{id} per card. Mobile 390 no overflow. LogIncident save-receipt-download-pdf testid exists and logic mirrors detail page (full save-then-download flow not end-to-end tested because it requires microphone/transcript flow, but code review confirms correctness).
